│ wrapping    │ that keeps the border   │ column separators     │ far longer than its header    │ █
 │             │ intact                  │                       │                               │ █
 ├─────────────┼─────────────────────────┼───────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤ █
 │ Terminal    │ Inherits the terminal   │ Inherits the          │ This is the row that proves   │ █
 │ background  │ background instead of   │ terminal background   │ background parity across the  │ █
 │ inheritance │ painting its own        │ instead of painting   │ two clients                   │ █
 │             │                         │ its own               │                               │ █
 ├─────────────┼─────────────────────────┼───────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────┤ █
 │ Fenced      │ Preserves indentation   │ Preserves             │ Long lines should be handled  │ █
 │ code block  │ and monospace           │ indentation and       │ the same way by both          │ █
 │ styling     │ alignment for every     │ monospace alignment   │ renderers                     │ █
 │             │ line                    │ for every line        │                               │ █
 ╰─────────────┴─────────────────────────┴───────────────────────┴───────────────────────────────╯ █
                                                                                                   █
     export const renderReference = (input: ReferenceInput): Effect.Effect<Rendered,               █
     RenderError> =>                                                                               █
       Effect.gen(function* () {                                                                   █
         const width = yield* Terminal.currentWidth                                                █
         const wrapped = wrapParagraph(input.paragraph, width)                                     ▇
         const table = layoutTable(input.rows, { border: "rounded", inheritBackground: true })     █
         return { headings: input.headings, paragraph: wrapped, table }                            █
       })                                                                                          █
                                                                                                   █
 The closing paragraph adds one more long wrapping line so the reviewer can compare the blank-row  █
 rhythm between the code block above and this final block of prose in both clients.                █
